message:
fix(context): narrow redirect-target visibility to a single token (echo/printf)

Fresh-eyes follow-up to the previous nqhi.4 sanitize change. The first
version exited args-data masking completely on a redirect operator,
which left ALL subsequent tokens visible — not just the redirect
target. That created a real false-positive vector:

    echo "data" > /var/log/app.log "rm -rf /"

Old behavior: `"data"` masked, then `>` exited args-data and the
trailing `"rm -rf /"` string-literal stayed visible. The
`rm-rf-root-home` regex then matched the literal `rm -rf /` inside the
quoted string and falsely blocked. Bash semantics: that string is just
echo's third argument — it's text being written to the log, not an
executed command.

New behavior: a single-token visibility window. When sanitize sees a
redirect operator while in args-data mode, it sets
`next_token_is_redirect_target = true`. The very next token is left
visible (the actual redirect target — the path the destructive rule
keys on). Subsequent tokens return to args-data masking.

Trace cases (verified with throwaway test):

  `echo > /etc/passwd`                      → `echo > /etc/passwd`
  `echo "x" > /etc/passwd`                  → `echo     > /etc/passwd`
  `echo "data" > /var/log/app.log "rm -rf /"`
    → `echo        > /var/log/app.log           ` (rm-rf MASKED, no FP)
  `echo data > /tmp/a 2> /etc/passwd`       → both targets visible
  `echo data > /tmp/a 2> /tmp/err "rm -rf /"`
    → both targets visible, trailing rm-rf MASKED (no FP)
  `echo $(rm -rf /tmp/foo) > /etc/passwd`   → inline code preserved (existing path)

The flag is also reset on shell separators (alongside the other
per-segment state) so a redirect at the end of one segment doesn't
leak visibility into the next.

All 2720 lib tests pass; full e2e harness 485/485; legacy e2e_test.sh
261/261; cargo fmt + clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
